                                                                                                                                            • DeepAgent: A General Reasoning Agent with Scalable Toolsets

                                                                                                                                            DeepAgent：一种具备可扩展工具集的通用推理智能体

                                                                                                                                            Xiaoxi Li* 李晓曦*

                                                                                                                                            Renmin University of China 中国人民大学

                                                                                                                                            Beijing, China 中国北京

                                                                                                                                            xiaoxi_li@ruc.edu.cn

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            Wenxiang Jiao 焦文祥

                                                                                                                                            Jiarui Jin 金嘉瑞

                                                                                                                                            Xiaohongshu Inc. 小红书科技有限公司

                                                                                                                                            Beijing, China 中国北京

                                                                                                                                            Guanting Dong 董冠廷

                                                                                                                                            Jiajie Jin 金佳杰

                                                                                                                                            Renmin University of China 中国人民大学

                                                                                                                                            Beijing, China 中国北京

                                                                                                                                            Yinuo Wang 王一诺

                                                                                                                                            Tsinghua University 清华大学

                                                                                                                                            Beijing, China 中国北京

                                                                                                                                            Hao Wang 王浩

                                                                                                                                            Xiaohongshu Inc. 小红书科技有限公司

                                                                                                                                            Beijing, China 中国北京

                                                                                                                                            Yutao Zhu 朱郁韬

                                                                                                                                            Renmin University of China 中国人民大学

                                                                                                                                            Beijing, China 中国北京

                                                                                                                                            Ji-Rong Wen 文继荣

                                                                                                                                            Renmin University of China 中国人民大学

                                                                                                                                            Beijing, China 中国北京

                                                                                                                                            jrwen@ruc.edu.cn

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            Yuan Lu 陆原

                                                                                                                                            Xiaohongshu Inc. 小红书科技有限公司

                                                                                                                                            Beijing, China 中国北京

                                                                                                                                            luyuan3@xiaohongshu.com

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            Zhicheng Dou † 窦志成 †

                                                                                                                                            Renmin University of China 中国人民大学

                                                                                                                                            Beijing, China 中国北京

                                                                                                                                            dou@ruc.edu.cn

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            Abstract

                                                                                                                                            摘要

                                                                                                                                            Large reasoning models have demonstrated strong problem-solving abilities, yet real-world tasks often require external tools and long-horizon interactions. Existing agent frameworks typically follow predefined workflows, which limit autonomous and global task completion. In this paper, we introduce DeepAgent, an end-to-end deep reasoning agent that performs autonomous thinking, tool discovery, and action execution within a single, coherent reasoning process. To manage long-horizon interactions, we introduce an autonomous memory folding mechanism that compresses past interactions into structured episodic, working, and tool memories, reducing error accumulation while preserving critical information. To teach general-purpose tool use efficiently and stably, we develop an end-to-end reinforcement learning strategy, namely ToolPO, that leverages LLM-simulated APIs and applies tool-call advantage attribution to assign fine-grained credit to the tool invocation tokens. Extensive experiments on eight benchmarks, including general tool-use tasks (ToolBench, API-Bank, TMDB, Spotify, ToolHop) and downstream applications (ALFWorld, WebShop, GAIA, HLE), demonstrate that DeepAgent consistently outperforms baselines across both labeled-tool and open-set tool retrieval scenarios. The code and demo are available at https://github.com/RUC-NLPIR/DeepAgent. 大型推理模型展现出了强大的问题解决能力，但现实任务往往需要外部工具和长程交互。现有的智能体框架通常遵循预定义的工作流，这限制了其自主完成全局任务的能力。在本文中，我们提出了 DeepAgent，这是一个端到端的深度推理智能体，能够在单一且连贯的推理过程中执行自主思考、工具发现和动作执行。为了管理长程交互，我们引入了一种自主记忆折叠机制，将过去的交互压缩为结构化的情景记忆、工作记忆和工具记忆，在减少错误累积的同时保留关键信息。为了高效且稳定地教授通用工具使用，我们开发了一种端到端强化学习策略，即 ToolPO，它利用大模型模拟的 API，并应用工具调用优势归因，为工具调用标记分配细粒度的信用。在包括通用工具使用任务（ToolBench、API-Bank、TMDB、Spotify、ToolHop）和下游应用（ALFWorld、WebShop、GAIA、HLE）在内的八个基准测试上的广泛实验表明，DeepAgent 在标记工具和开放集工具检索场景下均持续优于基准模型。代码和演示地址：https://github.com/RUC-NLPIR/DeepAgent。

                                                                                                                                            CCS Concepts

                                                                                                                                            CCS 概念

                                                                                                                                            • Computing methodologies → Planning and scheduling.

                                                                                                                                            • 计算方法 → 规划与调度。

                                                                                                                                            Keywords

                                                                                                                                            关键词

                                                                                                                                            Large Reasoning Models, Autonomous Agents, Tool Retrieval, Memory Mechanism, Reinforcement Learning 大型推理模型，自主智能体，工具检索，记忆机制，强化学习

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            Figure 1: Overall performance on (a) general tool usage tasks and (b) downstream applications (best score as 100%). 图 1：在 (a) 通用工具使用任务和 (b) 下游应用中的整体表现（最高分记为 100%）。

                                                                                                                                            ACM 引用格式：

DeepAgent: A General Reasoning Agent with Scalable Toolsets. In Proceedings of the ACM Web Conference 2026 (WWW '26), April 13-17, 2026, Dubai, United Arab Emirates. ACM, New York, NY, USA, 12 pages. https: //doi.org/10.1145/3774904.3792460 Xiaoxi Li, Wenxiang Jiao, Jiarui Jin, Guanting Dong, Jiajie Jin, Yinuo Wang, Hao Wang, Yutao Zhu, Ji-Rong Wen, Yuan Lu, and Zhicheng Dou. 2026. DeepAgent: A General Reasoning Agent with Scalable Toolsets. In Proceedings of the ACM Web Conference 2026 (WWW '26), April 13-17, 2026, Dubai, United Arab Emirates. ACM, New York, NY, USA, 12 pages. https: //doi.org/10.1145/3774904.3792460

                                                                                                                                            1 Introduction

                                                                                                                                            1 引言

                                                                                                                                            The rapid advancement of Large Language Models (LLMs) has inspired the development of LLM-powered agents, which have found broad applications in scenarios such as web information seeking, software engineering, and personal assistance [19, 39]. Most existing agents follow predefined workflows (e.g., ReAct [68] and Plan-and-Solve [52]) with iterative "Reason-Act-Observe" loops (Figure 2(a)). Although effective in simpler tasks, these approaches suffer from several critical limitations: (1) lack of autonomy in execution steps and overall procedure; (2) inability to dynamically discover tools during task execution; (3) deficiency in fully autonomous management of interactive memory; and (4) insufficient depth and coherence in reasoning about the entire task. These limitations hinder agents from real-world problems, particularly for complex tasks that demand general and multiple tool-use. 大型语言模型（LLMs）的飞速发展推动了 LLM 驱动智能体的开发，这些智能体已广泛应用于网络信息搜索、软件工程和个人助理等场景 [19, 39]。大多数现有智能体遵循预定义的流程（如 ReAct [68] 和 Plan-and-Solve [52]），采用迭代式的“推理-行动-观察”循环（图 2(a)）。尽管这些方法在简单任务中表现有效，但仍存在几个关键局限：(1) 执行步骤和整体流程缺乏自主性；(2) 无法在任务执行过程中动态发现工具；(3) 缺乏对交互式记忆的完全自主管理；(4) 对整个任务的推理深度和连贯性不足。这些局限性阻碍了智能体在现实世界问题中的应用，特别是在需要通用且多种工具协作的复杂任务中。

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            *Work done during internship at Xiaohongshu Inc. *工作完成于小红书科技有限公司实习期间。

                                                                                                                                            † Corresponding author. † 通讯作者。

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            Figure 2: Comparison of agent paradigms: (a) Traditional agents with predefined workflows, (b) Deep Research agents that can autonomously call limited tools, and (c) Our DeepAgent, a fully autonomous reasoning agent that dynamically discovers and invokes helpful tools, all within a continuous agentic reasoning process. 图 2：智能体范式对比：(a) 具有预定义工作流的传统智能体，(b) 可自主调用有限工具的深度研究智能体，以及 (c) 我们的 DeepAgent，一种在持续的智能体推理过程中动态发现并调用有用工具的完全自主推理智能体。

                                                                                                                                            Recently, the advent of Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) has demonstrated the capability to solve complex problems in domains like mathematics, programming, and scientific reasoning through a step-by-step "slow thinking" process [2, 30, 55]. However, many real-world tasks necessitate the use of external tools for their completion. Recent approaches integrate tool use into reasoning [25, 29, 72], but typically rely on a small, fixed tool set such as search, browsing, and coding (Figure 2(b)), limiting their generality. 近期，大型推理模型（LRMs）的出现展示了通过逐步“慢思考”过程解决数学、编程和科学推理等领域复杂问题的能力 [2, 30, 55]。然而，许多现实任务需要借助外部工具才能完成。现有研究将工具使用整合进推理过程 [25, 29, 72]，但通常依赖于搜索、浏览和编码等少量固定工具集（图 2(b)），限制了其通用性。

                                                                                                                                            To address these challenges, we introduce DeepAgent, an end-to-end deep reasoning agent that can complete an entire task by dynamically retrieving and calling tools within a single, coherent agentic reasoning process. As depicted in Figure 2(c), DeepAgent operates by autonomously thinking, searching for tools, and executing actions. This paradigm shifts away from traditional, predefined workflows that rely on predefined tools, task planning, and iterative tool use. Instead, DeepAgent maintains a global perspective on the entire task, unconstrained by the need to deliberate on specific, isolated operations. Tools are not pre-retrieved in advance but are dynamically discovered on an as-needed basis, thereby fully unlocking the autonomous potential of the large reasoning model. 为应对这些挑战，我们引入了 DeepAgent，这是一种端到端的深度推理智能体，能够在单一连贯的智能体推理过程中，通过动态检索和调用工具来完成整个任务。如图 2(c) 所示，DeepAgent 通过自主思考、搜索工具和执行行动来运作。这种范式摆脱了依赖预定义工具、任务规划和迭代式工具使用的传统预定义工作流。相反，DeepAgent 对整个任务保持全局视角，不受限于对特定孤立操作的审议。工具无需预先检索，而是根据需要动态发现，从而充分释放了大型推理模型的自主潜力。

                                                                                                                                            To facilitate robust exploration in long-horizon environments, we equip DeepAgent with Autonomous Memory Folding. This strategy allows the agent to dynamically consolidate its reasoning process and interaction history into a structured memory schema. Beyond reducing token overhead, this mechanism enables the agent to "take a breath"-pausing to reconsider strategies and avoid erroneous paths. To minimize information loss during consolidation, we introduce a brain-inspired memory architecture comprising episodic, working, and tool memory, all structured with an agent-usable data schema to ensure the stability and utility of the folded memory. 为了促进在长程环境中的稳健探索，我们为 DeepAgent 配备了自主记忆折叠（Autonomous Memory Folding）功能。该策略允许智能体将其推理过程和交互历史动态整合为结构化的记忆模式。除了降低 Token 开销外，该机制还使智能体能够“喘口气”——即暂停下来重新审视策略，避免走入错误路径。为最大限度减少整合过程中的信息丢失，我们引入了一种受大脑启发的记忆架构，包含情景记忆、工作记忆和工具记忆，并采用智能体可用的数据模式进行结构化，以确保折叠记忆的稳定性和实用性。

                                                                                                                                            To enhance DeepAgent's proficiency in mastering these mechanisms, we propose ToolPO, an end-to-end reinforcement learning (RL) training method tailored for general tool use. Existing agentic RL training in general domains presents two significant challenges: (1) The reliance on a multitude of real-world APIs during training can lead to instability, slow execution, and high costs. To prevent this, we leverage LLM-simulated APIs, which enhance the stability and efficiency of the training process. (2) A sparse reward based solely on the final outcome is often insufficient to guarantee the accuracy of intermediate tool calls. We address this by implementing tool-call advantage attribution, which precisely assigns credit to the specific tokens responsible for correct tool invocations, thereby providing a more granular and effective learning signal. 为提升 DeepAgent 掌握这些机制的熟练度，我们提出了 ToolPO，这是一种专为通用工具使用而设计的端到端强化学习（RL）训练方法。现有通用领域的智能体强化学习训练面临两大挑战：(1) 训练过程中对大量现实世界 API 的依赖可能导致不稳定、执行缓慢且成本高昂。为避免这种情况，我们利用 LLM 模拟的 API，提高了训练过程的稳定性和效率。(2) 仅基于最终结果的稀疏奖励通常不足以保证中间工具调用的准确性。我们通过实施工具调用优势归因（tool-call advantage attribution）来解决这一问题，该方法能精确地将贡献度分配给负责正确工具调用的特定 Token，从而提供更细粒度且有效的学习信号。

                                                                                                                                            We conduct extensive experiments on a wide range of benchmarks. For (1) General Tool-Use Tasks, we evaluate DeepAgent on ToolBench, API-Bank, TMDB, Spotify, and ToolHop, which feature toolsets scaling from tens to over ten thousand distinct tools. For (2) Downstream Applications, we test its performance on ALFWorld, WebShop, GAIA, and Humanity's Last Exam (HLE), which require the use of domain-specific toolsets. The overall results in Figure 1 show that DeepAgent achieves superior performance across all scenarios. 我们在广泛的基准测试上进行了大量实验。针对 (1) 通用工具使用任务，我们在 ToolBench、API-Bank、TMDB、Spotify 和 ToolHop 上评估了 DeepAgent，这些任务的工具集规模从数十个到超过一万个不等。针对 (2) 下游应用，我们在 ALFWorld、WebShop、GAIA 和 Humanity's Last Exam (HLE) 上测试了其性能，这些任务需要使用特定领域的工具集。图 1 中的总体结果表明，DeepAgent 在所有场景中均实现了卓越的性能。

                                                                                                                                            Our main contributions are summarized as follows: 我们的主要贡献总结如下：

                                                                                                                                            (1) We propose DeepAgent, the first agentic framework that enables reasoning models to autonomously think, discover tools, and execute actions within a unified reasoning process, empowering LRMs to harness toolsets of arbitrary scale and generalize to complex real-world tasks. (1) 我们提出了 DeepAgent，这是首个使推理模型能够在统一推理过程中自主思考、发现工具并执行动作的智能体框架，赋能大型推理模型 (LRM) 利用任意规模的工具集，并泛化至复杂的现实世界任务。

                                                                                                                                            (2) We introduce an autonomous memory folding mechanism, complemented by a brain-inspired memory design. This endows the agent with the ability to "take a breath" and reconsider its exploration strategies following unsuccessful attempts. (2) 我们引入了一种自主记忆折叠机制，并辅以受大脑启发的记忆设计。这赋予了智能体在尝试失败后“喘息”并重新审视其探索策略的能力。

                                                                                                                                            (3) We propose an end-to-end reinforcement learning training methodology for general-purpose tool use, ensuring stability and efficiency in large-scale tool execution during training, as well as accuracy in tool invocation during reasoning. (3) 我们提出了一种用于通用工具使用的端到端强化学习训练方法，确保了训练期间大规模工具执行的稳定性和效率，以及推理过程中工具调用的准确性。

                                                                                                                                            (4) We conduct extensive experiments across eight benchmarks, demonstrating DeepAgent's superior tool-use capabilities and high adaptability to real-world tasks. (4) 我们在八个基准测试上进行了广泛实验，证明了 DeepAgent 卓越的工具使用能力及其对现实世界任务的高度适应性。

                                                                                                                                            2 Related Work

                                                                                                                                            2 相关工作

                                                                                                                                            2.1 Large Reasoning Models

                                                                                                                                            2.1 大型推理模型

                                                                                                                                            Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) [4, 16] have demonstrated significant performance improvements in mathematical, scientific, and coding tasks by employing step-by-step slow thinking processes before generating final responses. Existing research has explored various approaches to elicit extended Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning [60] from models, including data synthesis for Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) [36, 54], and end-to-end RL [4]. Additionally, substantial work has investigated optimization strategies for reasoning models, such as advanced RL training algorithms [58] and improving reasoning efficiency [66]. However, models relying solely on parametric knowledge face inherent limitations and cannot interact with the real world. Recent studies have begun exploring tool-augmented reasoning approaches, including Search-o1 [25], Search-R1 [18], ToRL [29], DeepResearcher [72], and SimpleTIR [64]. However, these methods typically support only a limited set of research-oriented tools, such as web search, page browsing, and code execution, which constrains their applicability to real-world scenarios that demand access to more diverse tools. 大型推理模型 (LRM) [4, 16] 通过在生成最终响应前采用逐步的慢思考过程，在数学、科学和编码任务中展现了显著的性能提升。现有研究探索了多种激发模型进行扩展思维链 (CoT) [60] 推理的方法，包括用于监督微调 (SFT) 的数据合成 [36, 54] 以及端到端强化学习 [4]。此外，大量工作研究了推理模型的优化策略，例如先进的强化学习训练算法 [58] 和提高推理效率 [66]。然而，仅依赖参数化知识的模型面临固有局限，无法与现实世界交互。近期研究已开始探索工具增强的推理方法，包括 Search-o1 [25]、Search-R1 [18]、ToRL [29]、DeepResearcher [72] 和 SimpleTIR [64]。然而，这些方法通常仅支持有限的一组研究导向型工具（如网络搜索、页面浏览和代码执行），这限制了它们在需要访问更多样化工具的现实场景中的适用性。

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            Figure 3: Overview of the DeepAgent framework. The main reasoning model autonomously discovers tools, executes actions, and folds previous memory to restart with structured memories, all within a unified thinking process. The DeepAgent is trained end-to-end with ToolPO, an RL method that uses a tool simulator to simulate large-scale real-world tool APIs, and rewards both final task success and correct intermediate tool calls through fine-grained advantage attribution. 图 3：DeepAgent 框架概览。主推理模型在统一的思维过程中自主发现工具、执行动作，并折叠既往记忆以利用结构化记忆重新开始。DeepAgent 通过 ToolPO 进行端到端训练，这是一种利用工具模拟器来模拟大规模现实世界工具 API 的强化学习方法，并通过细粒度的优势归因奖励最终任务成功和正确的中间工具调用。

                                                                                                                                            2.2 Autonomous Agents

                                                                                                                                            2.2 自主智能体

                                                                                                                                            LLM-powered autonomous agents accomplish real-world tasks by invoking external tools to interact with their environment [6,7,13− 15, 20, 22, 27, 28, 31, 38, 46, 49, 57, 61, 71]. Current agent methodologies, including ReAct [68], Plan-and-Solve [52], Reflextion [44], and CodeAct [56], predominantly follow predefined workflows with fixed execution patterns. This rigid structure limits their ability to fully leverage the autonomous decision-making and deep reasoning capabilities of advanced reasoning models. Recent efforts have investigated training LLMs to autonomously invoke tools through data synthesis and SFT methods [9, 63] and RL training frameworks [3, 5, 8, 10, 17, 23, 32, 48, 59]. However, most existing methods rely on pre-selected, labeled tools, which limit their applicability to real-world scenarios. Real-world tasks are highly variable and require access to diverse toolsets that cannot be predetermined, aligning with the emerging Model Context Protocol (MCP) [12] paradigm. Although some prior work has explored tool retrieval mechanisms [37, 42, 53], most approaches conduct only a single upfront retrieval step and incorporate the retrieved tools, with limited exploration of dynamic tool discovery during task execution. Therefore, we aim to develop a deep reasoning agent capable of dynamically discovering and invoking helpful tools from scalable toolsets to address more generalized real-world tasks. 基于大语言模型的自主智能体通过调用外部工具与环境交互来完成现实世界任务 [6,7,13− 15, 20, 22, 27, 28, 31, 38, 46, 49, 57, 61, 71]。当前的智能体方法，包括 ReAct [68]、Plan-and-Solve [52]、Reflextion [44] 和 CodeAct [56]，主要遵循具有固定执行模式的预定义工作流。这种僵化的结构限制了它们充分利用先进推理模型的自主决策和深度推理能力。近期工作研究了通过数据合成和 SFT 方法 [9, 63] 以及强化学习训练框架 [3, 5, 8, 10, 17, 23, 32, 48, 59] 来训练大语言模型自主调用工具。然而，大多数现有方法依赖于预先选择和标记的工具，这限制了它们在现实场景中的适用性。现实世界任务高度多变，需要访问无法预先确定的多样化工具集，这与新兴的模型上下文协议 (MCP) [12] 范式相一致。尽管一些先前工作探索了工具检索机制 [37, 42, 53]，但大多数方法仅执行单次预检索步骤并整合检索到的工具，对任务执行过程中动态工具发现的探索有限。因此，我们旨在开发一种深度推理智能体，能够从可扩展的工具集中动态发现并调用有用的工具，以解决更通用的现实世界任务。

                                                                                                                                            3 Methodology

                                                                                                                                            3 方法论

                                                                                                                                            3.1 Problem Formulation

                                                                                                                                            3.1 问题形式化

                                                                                                                                            We frame the agent's task as a sequential decision-making process. The agent receives a user-provided question Q and an instruction I , and interacts with an environment over a series of steps t=1,…,T to accomplish the specified goal. The environment provides access to a collection of tools T at an arbitrary scale. 我们将智能体的任务构建为一个序列决策过程。智能体接收用户提供的问题 Q 和指令 I，并通过一系列步骤 t=1,…,T 与环境交互以实现既定目标。环境提供对任意规模工具集合 T 的访问权限。

                                                                                                                                            At each step t ,the agent’s state st consists of the history of all previous actions and their resulting observations,i.e., st=(a1,o1,…,at−1,ot−1) . The agent,driven by a policy π parameterized by θ ,selects an action at based on the current state,the user question, and the instruction: 在每个步骤 t 中，智能体的状态 st 由之前所有动作及其产生的结果观察组成，即 st=(a1,o1,…,at−1,ot−1)。智能体在由 θ 参数化的策略 π 驱动下，根据当前状态、用户问题和指令选择一个动作 at：

                                                                                                                                            (1)at∼πθ(⋅∣st,Q,I).

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            An action at can be one of four types: 动作 at 可分为四种类型：

                                                                                                                                            • Internal Thought (at think ) : A textual reasoning step generated by the LRM to analyze the problem or plan its next steps. The corresponding observation ot is typically empty.

                                                                                                                                            • 内部思考 (at think )：由 LRM 生成的文本推理步骤，用于分析问题或规划后续步骤。相应的观察 ot 通常为空。

                                                                                                                                            • Tool Search (at search ) : A natural language query qs to find relevant tools from T . The observation ot is a list of retrieved tools.

                                                                                                                                            • 工具搜索 (at search )：用于从 T 中查找相关工具的自然语言查询 qs。观察 ot 为检索到的工具列表。

                                                                                                                                            • Tool Call (at call ) : The invocation of a specific tool τ∈T with a set of arguments. The observation ot is the execution result returned by the tool.

                                                                                                                                            • 工具调用 (at call )：调用带有特定参数的工具 τ∈T。观察 ot 为工具返回的执行结果。

                                                                                                                                            • Memory Fold ( at fold  ): A special action to compress the interaction history st into a structured memory summary. The subsequent state st+1 is then initialized with this compressed memory.

                                                                                                                                            • 记忆折叠 ( at fold  )：一种将交互历史 st 压缩为结构化记忆摘要的特殊动作。随后的状态 st+1 将以此压缩后的记忆进行初始化。

                                                                                                                                            The sequence of states, actions, and observations forms a trajectory τ=(s1,a1,o1,…,sT,aT,oT) . The process terminates when the agent completes the task or reaches a maximum step limit. Suppose R(τ) is a reward function that evaluates the overall success of the trajectory τ ,the objective is to learn an optimal policy πθ∗ that maximizes the expected cumulative reward for a given task: 状态、动作和观察的序列构成一条轨迹 τ=(s1,a1,o1,…,sT,aT,oT)。当智能体完成任务或达到最大步数限制时，过程终止。假设 R(τ) 是评估轨迹 τ 整体成功率的奖励函数，目标是学习一个最优策略 πθ∗，以最大化给定任务的期望累积奖励：

                                                                                                                                            (2)πθ∗=arg⁡maxπθ⁡Eτ∼πθ[R(τ)].

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            3.2 Overview of the DeepAgent Framework

                                                                                                                                            3.2 DeepAgent 框架概览

                                                                                                                                            As illustrated in Figure 3, the DeepAgent framework is architected around a main reasoning process, which is supported by several auxiliary mechanisms to ensure robustness and efficiency. 如图 3 所示，DeepAgent 框架围绕一个核心推理过程构建，并辅以多种辅助机制以确保系统的稳健性和效率。

                                                                                                                                            • Main Reasoning Process: The core of DeepAgent is a powerful large reasoning model that drives the entire task-completion process. In a single stream of thought, the LRM autonomously reasons about the task, dynamically discovers necessary tools, executes actions, and manages its own memory. This unified approach departs from traditional, rigid agent workflows, allowing the LRM to maintain a global perspective on the task.

                                                                                                                                            • 核心推理过程：DeepAgent 的核心是一个强大的大型推理模型（LRM），它驱动整个任务完成过程。在单一的思维流中，LRM 自主地对任务进行推理、动态发现所需工具、执行动作并管理自身记忆。这种统一的方法摒弃了传统僵化的智能体工作流，使 LRM 能够保持对任务的全局视角。

                                                                                                                                            • Auxiliary Mechanisms: DeepAgent employs an auxiliary LLM to handle complex interactions with large toolsets and manage long histories. This background model enhances system stability by: (1) filtering and summarizing retrieved tool documentation if it's too lengthy, (2) denoising and condensing verbose information returned from tool calls, and (3) compressing long interaction histories into a structured memory. This division of labor allows the main LRM to concentrate on high-level strategic reasoning.

                                                                                                                                            • 辅助机制：DeepAgent 采用一个辅助 LLM 来处理与大规模工具集的复杂交互并管理长历史记录。该后台模型通过以下方式增强系统稳定性：(1) 在工具文档过长时进行过滤和摘要，(2) 对工具调用返回的冗长信息进行去噪和精简，以及 (3) 将长交互历史压缩为结构化记忆。这种分工使核心 LRM 能够专注于高层战略推理。

                                                                                                                                            3.3 Autonomous Tool Search and Calling

                                                                                                                                            3.3 自主工具搜索与调用

                                                                                                                                            DeepAgent's main LRM performs all actions by generating specific textual prompts within its continuous reasoning process. These actions are then intercepted and executed by the system. DeepAgent 的核心 LRM 通过在其连续推理过程中生成特定的文本提示来执行所有动作。这些动作随后会被系统拦截并执行。

                                                                                                                                            Tool Search. When the agent determines it needs a tool, it generates a tool search query qs encapsulated within special tokens: <tool_search> qs </tool_search>. The system's tool retriever operates via dense retrieval. First, we build an index by pre-computing an embedding E(di) for the documentation di of each tool τi∈T using an embedding model E . During inference,given the query qs , the system retrieves the top- k tools by ranking them based on the cosine similarity sim(⋅,⋅) : 工具搜索。当智能体确定需要工具时，它会生成一个封装在特殊标记中的工具搜索查询 qs：<tool_search> qs </tool_search>。系统的工具检索器通过稠密检索运行。首先，我们通过使用嵌入模型 E 为每个工具 τi∈T 的文档 di 预计算嵌入 E(di) 来构建索引。在推理过程中，给定查询 qs，系统通过基于余弦相似度 sim(⋅,⋅) 对工具进行排序，检索出前 k 个工具：

                                                                                                                                            (3)T retrieved =top-kτi∈T⁡(sim(E(qs),E(di))).

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            The retrieved tool documentation is then processed by the auxiliary LLM -summarized if too lengthy, otherwise provided directly-and returned to the main LRM's context: <tool_search_result> relevant tools </tool_search_result>. 检索到的工具文档随后由辅助大语言模型进行处理（如果过长则进行摘要，否则直接提供），并返回给主大语言推理模型（LRM）的上下文：<tool_search_result> 相关工具 </tool_search_result>。

                                                                                                                                            Tool Call. To execute a tool, the agent generates a structured call including the tool's name and arguments: <tool_call> {"name": "tool_name", "arguments": ...}</tool_call>. The framework parses this call, executes the tool, and captures the output. This output is, if necessary, summarized by the auxiliary LLM to ensure it is concise and helpful, before being fed back into the reasoning context: <tool_call_result> helpful information </tool_call_result>. 工具调用。为了执行工具，智能体生成一个包含工具名称和参数的结构化调用：<tool_call> {"name": "tool_name", "arguments": ...}</tool_call>。框架解析此调用、执行工具并捕获输出。如有必要，该输出会由辅助大语言模型进行摘要，以确保其简洁且有帮助，然后再反馈回推理上下文中：<tool_call_result> 有用信息 </tool_call_result>。

                                                                                                                                            3.4 Autonomous Memory Folding and Brain-Inspired Memory Schema

                                                                                                                                            3.4 自主记忆折叠与类脑记忆模式

                                                                                                                                            The agent can trigger memory folding at any logical point in its reasoning process-such as after completing a sub-task or realizing an exploration path was incorrect-by generating a special token: <fold_thought>. Upon detecting this token, the system initiates the memory folding process. The auxiliary LLM (parameterized by θ aux  ) processes the entire preceding interaction history st and generates three structured memory components in parallel: 智能体可以在其推理过程的任何逻辑节点触发记忆折叠——例如在完成子任务后或意识到探索路径错误时——通过生成特殊标记：<fold_thought>。检测到此标记后，系统启动记忆折叠过程。辅助大语言模型（由 θ aux  参数化）处理整个先前的交互历史 st，并并行生成三个结构化记忆组件：

                                                                                                                                            (4)(ME,MW,MT)=f compress (st;θ aux ).

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            These compressed episodic (ME) ,working (MW) ,and tool (MT) memories then replace the raw interaction history, enabling the agent to proceed with a refreshed and condensed view of its progress while avoiding entrapment in incorrect exploration paths. 这些压缩后的情景记忆 (ME)、工作记忆 (MW) 和工具记忆 (MT) 随后会替换原始交互历史，使智能体能够以更新且精简的视角继续推进任务，同时避免陷入错误的探索路径。

                                                                                                                                            Inspired by human cognitive systems, the structured memory Mt is composed of three distinct components that are generated in parallel: Mt=(ME,MW,MT) ,where ME,MW,MT denote episodic, working, and tool memories, respectively. 受人类认知系统启发，结构化记忆 Mt 由三个并行生成的不同组件组成：Mt=(ME,MW,MT)，其中 ME,MW,MT 分别表示情景记忆、工作记忆和工具记忆。

                                                                                                                                            • Episodic Memory (ME) : This component serves as a high-level log of the task, recording key events, major decision points, and sub-task completions. It provides the agent with long-term context regarding the overall task structure and its overarching goals.

                                                                                                                                            • 情景记忆 (ME)：该组件作为任务的高级日志，记录关键事件、主要决策点和子任务完成情况。它为智能体提供有关整体任务结构及其宏观目标的长期上下文。

                                                                                                                                            • Working Memory (MW) : This contains the most recent information, such as the current sub-goal, obstacles encountered, and near-term plans. It is the core component that ensures the continuity of the agent's reasoning across the memory fold.

                                                                                                                                            • 工作记忆 (MW)：包含最近的信息，如当前子目标、遇到的障碍和近期计划。它是确保智能体在记忆折叠后推理连续性的核心组件。

                                                                                                                                            • Tool Memory (MT) : This consolidates all tool-related interactions, including which tools have been used, how they were invoked, and their effectiveness. It allows the agent to learn from its experiences, refining its tool selection and usage strategies.

                                                                                                                                            • 工具记忆 (MT)：整合了所有与工具相关的交互，包括已使用的工具、调用方式及其有效性。它使智能体能够从经验中学习，从而优化其工具选择和使用策略。

                                                                                                                                            To ensure that the compressed memory is stable and easily parsed by the agent, we employ an agent-usable data schema in JSON format instead of unstructured natural language. It offers two main benefits: maintaining a controllable and predictable structure, and mitigating the loss of critical details that can occur when summarizing long-form text. Details of the data schema are in Appendix C. 为确保压缩后的记忆稳定且易于被智能体解析，我们采用 JSON 格式的智能体可用数据模式，而非非结构化的自然语言。它具有两大优势：保持结构的可控性和可预测性，并减少在总结长文本时可能发生的关键细节丢失。数据模式的详细信息见附录 C。

                                                                                                                                            3.5 End-to-end RL Training with ToolPO

                                                                                                                                            3.5 基于 ToolPO 的端到端强化学习训练

                                                                                                                                            We train DeepAgent end-to-end with Tool Policy Optimization (ToolPO), an RL approach designed for general tool-using agents. 我们使用工具策略优化（ToolPO）对 DeepAgent 进行端到端训练，这是一种专为通用工具使用智能体设计的强化学习方法。

                                                                                                                                            Training Data Collection. We first collect a diverse training dataset spanning four categories. To instill general tool-use capabilities, we use ToolBench [37]. For real-world interaction, we leverage ALFWorld [45] and WebShop [67]. To enhance deep research skills, we incorporate data from WebDancer [61] and WebShaperQA [50]. Lastly, to improve mathematical reasoning with code, we use DeepMath [11]. Further details are available in Appendix A.1. 训练数据收集。我们首先收集了一个涵盖四个类别的多样化训练数据集。为灌输通用工具使用能力，我们使用了 ToolBench [37]。对于现实世界交互，我们利用了 ALFWorld [45] 和 WebShop [67]。为增强深度研究技能，我们整合了来自 WebDancer [61] 和 WebShaperQA [50] 的数据。最后，为提升代码辅助的数学推理能力，我们使用了 DeepMath [11]。更多详细信息请参阅附录 A.1。

                                                                                                                                            Tool Simulator. Training an agent that interacts with thousands of real-world APIs is often impractical due to instability, latency, and cost. To address this, we develop an LLM-based Tool Simulator. 工具模拟器。由于不稳定性、延迟和成本问题，训练一个与数千个真实世界 API 交互的智能体往往不切实际。为解决此问题，我们开发了一个基于大语言模型的工具模拟器。

                                                                                                                                            Table 1: Main results on general tool usage tasks, encompassing scenarios with both labeled tools and open-set tool retrieval over large-scale toolsets. We report Pass@1 metric for all tasks. For 32B models, the best results are in bold and the second are underlined. Results from larger or closed-sourced models are in gray color for reference. 表 1：通用工具使用任务的主要结果，涵盖了标记工具场景及大规模工具集上的开放集工具检索场景。我们报告了所有任务的 Pass@1 指标。对于 32B 模型，最佳结果以粗体显示，次佳结果加下划线。来自更大规模或闭源模型的结果以灰色显示，仅供参考。

                                                                                                                                            MethodBackboneToolBenchAPI-BankTMDBSpotifyToolHop
                                                                                                                                            SuccessPathSuccessPathSuccessPathSuccessPathCorrectPath

                                                                                                                                            Workflow-based Methods

                                                                                                                                            CodeActQwen2.5-32B53.068.362.470.648.067.433.358.734.748.8

                                                                                                                                            Workflow-based Methods

                                                                                                                                            场景 1：使用真值工具完成任务
                                                                                                                                            基于工作流的方法
                                                                                                                                            ReActQwen2.5-32B41.064.760.468.346.065.329.856.337.649.1
                                                                                                                                            CodeActQwen2.5-32B53.068.362.470.648.067.433.358.734.748.8

                                                                                                                                            DeepAgent-32B-BaseQwQ-32B63.074.376.281.085.092.070.289.349.159.8

                                                                                                                                            场景 2：使用开放集工具检索完成任务
                                                                                                                                            基于工作流的方法
                                                                                                                                            ReActQwen2.5-32B55.020.816.042.011.034.57.025.413.217.9
                                                                                                                                            CodeActQwen2.5-32B51.019.022.049.619.046.810.531.612.717.4
                                                                                                                                            Plan-and-SolveQwen2.5-32B54.020.418.042.815.040.58.826.312.016.3
                                                                                                                                            ReActQwQ-32B44.019.020.052.718.040.322.845.527.122.3

                                                                                                                                            Plan-and-SolveQwQ-32B45.019.618.044.324.046.819.342.725.720.8

                                                                                                                                            ReActGPT-4o41.028.918.042.835.056.817.526.324.128.6
                                                                                                                                            ReActDeepSeek-R147.022.312.057.334.053.129.851.736.232.9
                                                                                                                                            推理过程中的自主工具检索与使用
                                                                                                                                            DeepAgent-32B-BaseQwQ-32B60.035.722.061.852.071.849.168.638.440.3
                                                                                                                                            DeepAgent-32B-RLQwQ-32B64.037.224.064.955.074.350.974.440.640.5

                                                                                                                                            This simulator, powered by an auxiliary LLM, mimics the responses of real-world APIs (e.g., RapidAPI). This approach provides a stable, efficient, and low-cost environment for robust RL training. 该模拟器由辅助大模型驱动，旨在模拟真实世界 API（如 RapidAPI）的响应。这种方法为稳健的强化学习训练提供了一个稳定、高效且低成本的环境。

                                                                                                                                            Global and Tool-Call Advantage Attribution. For each input prompt, we sample a group of K trajectories {τ1,…,τK} . ToolPO defines two distinct reward components. The first is a reward for overall task success, R succ (τ) ,which is a task-success score that reflects the quality of the final outcome (e.g., the accuracy of the final answer). The second is a tool-call reward, R action (τ) ,which reflects the quality of intermediate actions. This action-level reward is composed of rewards for correct tool invocations and efficient memory folding. Specifically, R action (τ)=λ1∑t=1T⁡C(at call )+λ2S pref (τ) ,where C(at call ) is 1 if a tool call is correct and 0 otherwise. S pref (τ) is a preference score encouraging efficient use of memory folding, defined by comparing a trajectory with folding (τ fold ) to one without (τ direct ):S pref =(L(τ direct )−L(τ fold ))/(L(τ direct )+L(τ fold )) . 全局与工具调用优势归因。对于每个输入提示，我们采样一组 K 轨迹 {τ1,…,τK}。ToolPO 定义了两个不同的奖励组件。第一个是整体任务成功奖励 R succ (τ)，即反映最终结果质量（例如最终答案的准确性）的任务成功分数。第二个是工具调用奖励 R action (τ)，反映中间动作的质量。该动作级奖励由正确工具调用奖励和高效内存折叠奖励组成。具体而言，R action (τ)=λ1∑t=1T⁡C(at call )+λ2S pref (τ)，其中 C(at call ) 在工具调用正确时为 1，否则为 0。S pref (τ) 是鼓励高效使用内存折叠的偏好分数，通过比较带有折叠 (τ fold ) 的轨迹与不带折叠 (τ direct ):S pref =(L(τ direct )−L(τ fold ))/(L(τ direct )+L(τ fold )) 的轨迹来定义。

                                                                                                                                            Based on these rewards, we compute two separate group-relative advantages. The task success advantage for trajectory τk is: 基于这些奖励，我们计算两个独立的组相对优势。轨迹 τk 的任务成功优势为：

                                                                                                                                            (5)A succ (τk)=R succ (τk)−1K∑j=1K⁡R succ (τj),

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            which is attributed to all generated tokens in τk ,providing a global learning signal. Similarly, the action-level advantage is: 该优势归因于 τk 中生成的所有标记，提供了一个全局学习信号。类似地，动作级优势为：

                                                                                                                                            (6)A action (τk)=R action (τk)−1K∑j=1K⁡R action (τj).

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            Crucially, this advantage is attributed only to the specific tokens that constitute the tool call and memory folding actions. This fine-grained credit assignment provides a more targeted signal for learning correct and efficient tool use. 关键在于，该优势仅归因于构成工具调用和内存折叠动作的特定标记。这种细粒度的信用分配为学习正确且高效的工具使用提供了更具针对性的信号。

                                                                                                                                            Optimization Objective. The total advantage for a given token yi in trajectory τk is the sum of the global and local advantages: 优化目标。给定轨迹 τk 中标记 yi 的总优势是全局优势与局部优势之和：

                                                                                                                                            (7)A(yi)=A succ (τk)+M(yi)⋅A action (τk),

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            where M(yi) is a mask that is 1 if yi is part of a tool-call or memory-fold token sequence, and 0 otherwise. ToolPO then optimizes the policy using a clipped surrogate objective function: 其中 M(yi) 是一个掩码，若 yi 属于工具调用或内存折叠标记序列，则为 1，否则为 0。随后，ToolPO 使用裁剪代理目标函数优化策略：

                                                                                                                                            L ToolPO (θ)=

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            (8)Eτk[∑i=1|τk|⁡min(ρi(θ)A(yi),clip(ρi(θ),1−ϵ,1+ϵ)A(yi))].

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            Here, ρi(θ)=πθ(yi∣y<i,s)πθ old (yi∣y<i,s) is the probability ratio for token yi . This objective encourages the model to increase the probability of both intermediate actions and end-to-end task accomplishment that exhibit positive relative advantage, thereby ensuring stable and effective policy updates. 此处，ρi(θ)=πθ(yi∣y<i,s)πθ old (yi∣y<i,s) 是标记 yi 的概率比。该目标函数鼓励模型增加那些表现出正相对优势的中间动作和端到端任务完成的概率，从而确保策略更新的稳定与有效。

                                                                                                                                            Table 2: Main results on downstream task applications, spanning Embodied AI (ALFWorld), Online Shopping (WebShop), General AI Assistants (GAIA), and Humanity's Last Exam (HLE). We report Pass@1 for all tasks. For 32B models, the best results are in bold and the second are underlined. Results from larger or closed-sourced models are in gray color for reference. 表 2：下游任务应用的主要结果，涵盖具身智能 (ALFWorld)、在线购物 (WebShop)、通用 AI 助手 (GAIA) 以及人类终极考试 (HLE)。我们报告所有任务的 Pass@1。对于 32B 模型，最佳结果以粗体显示，次佳结果加下划线。来自更大规模或闭源模型的结果以灰色显示，仅供参考。

                                                                                                                                            MethodBackboneALFWorldWebShopGAIAHLE
                                                                                                                                            SuccessPathSuccessScoreTextMMFileAllTextMMAll
                                                                                                                                            Completing Tasks w/ Task-specific Toolsets
                                                                                                                                            Workflow-based Methods

                                                                                                                                            4 Experimental Settings

                                                                                                                                            4 实验设置

                                                                                                                                            4.1 Tasks and Datasets

                                                                                                                                            4.1 任务与数据集

                                                                                                                                            We conduct extensive experiments on a wide range of benchmarks, including general tool-use and downstream applications. 我们针对广泛的基准测试进行了大量实验，涵盖通用工具使用及下游应用。

                                                                                                                                            General Tool-Use. These benchmarks cover toolsets from tens to >10k tools,and thus stress scalability. They evaluate core capabilities for general tool use, including tool planning, tool retrieval, and accurate multi-step tool calling. We use ToolBench [37] (16k+ real-world APIs; G3 subset with multi-step/multi-tool calls), API-Bank [24] (314 dialogues; 73 APIs; 753 calls), RestBench [47] (TMDB: 54 tools, 2.3 calls/question; Spotify: 40 tools, 2.6 calls/question), and ToolHop [69] (3,912 executable tools; 3-7 calls/task). We evaluate two settings: provided ground-truth tools and open-set tool retrieval from the full toolset. 通用工具使用。这些基准测试涵盖了从数十个到 >10k 个工具的工具集，旨在强调可扩展性。它们评估了通用工具使用的核心能力，包括工具规划、工具检索以及精确的多步工具调用。我们使用了 ToolBench [37]（1.6万+个真实世界 API；包含多步/多工具调用的 G3 子集）、API-Bank [24]（314 个对话；73 个 API；753 次调用）、RestBench [47]（TMDB：54 个工具，每个问题 2.3 次调用；Spotify：40 个工具，每个问题 2.6 次调用）以及 ToolHop [69]（3,912 个可执行工具；每个任务 3-7 次调用）。我们评估了两种设置：提供真值工具（ground-truth tools）和从完整工具集中进行开放集工具检索。

                                                                                                                                            Downstream Applications. We also evaluate downstream applications with domain-specific toolsets: ALFWorld [45] (text embodied tasks with nine actions, e.g., move/take), WebShop [67] (shopping with 'search' and 'click'), GAIA [33] (web search/browsing, VQA, code, file reading), and Humanity's Last Exam (HLE) [35] (code, search, browsing, VQA). These tasks test long-horizon interaction in more realistic environments, requiring state tracking, error recovery, and coordination across heterogeneous tools; we equip agents with task-specific toolsets. 下游应用。我们还评估了具有特定领域工具集的下游应用：ALFWorld [45]（包含九种动作的文本具身任务，如移动/拾取）、WebShop [67]（通过“搜索”和“点击”进行购物）、GAIA [33]（网络搜索/浏览、VQA、代码、文件读取）以及 Humanity's Last Exam (HLE) [35]（代码、搜索、浏览、VQA）。这些任务在更真实的场景中测试长程交互，需要状态跟踪、错误恢复以及跨异构工具的协调；我们为智能体配备了特定任务的工具集。

                                                                                                                                            4.2 Baselines

                                                                                                                                            4.2 基线方法

                                                                                                                                            Our baselines include: (1) Workflow-based Methods: ReAct [68] alternates explicit reasoning with environment actions in a Reason-Act-Observe loop. CodeAct [56] expresses actions as executable Python code that runs in an interpreter. Plan-and-Solve [52] first sketches a high-level plan and then executes it step by step. Reflexion [43] enhances learning through verbal self-reflection after failed attempts. AgentLM [70] uses instruction tuning to enhance general agent capabilities of LLMs. (2) Autonomous Tool Usage within Reasoning: WebThinker [26] interleaves thinking with web search and deep web exploration. HiRA [21] introduces a hierarchical agent architecture where a meta planner decomposes tasks, a coordinator routes subtasks, and specialized executors solve them with dual-channel memory. OpenAI Deep Research [34] is an agentic system based on reasoning models. 我们的基线方法包括：(1) 基于工作流的方法：ReAct [68] 在“推理-行动-观察”循环中交替进行显式推理与环境交互。CodeAct [56] 将动作表示为在解释器中运行的可执行 Python 代码。Plan-and-Solve [52] 先勾勒出高层计划，然后逐步执行。Reflexion [43] 通过失败尝试后的口头自我反思来增强学习。AgentLM [70] 利用指令微调来增强大语言模型的通用智能体能力。(2) 推理过程中的自主工具使用：WebThinker [26] 将思考与网络搜索及深度网页探索交织在一起。HiRA [21] 引入了一种分层智能体架构，其中元规划器分解任务，协调器分配子任务，专门的执行器通过双通道记忆解决任务。OpenAI Deep Research [34] 是一种基于推理模型的智能体系统。

                                                                                                                                            4.3 Implementation Details

                                                                                                                                            4.3 实现细节

                                                                                                                                            We use QwQ-32B [51] as DeepAgent's backbone model, with Qwen2.5- 32B-Instruct [40] as the auxiliary model in our main results. Text generation employs a maximum of 81,920 tokens with temperature 0.7, top_p 0.8, top_k 20, and repetition penalty 1.05. Web search and page browsing are implemented using Google Serper API and 我们使用 QwQ-32B [51] 作为 DeepAgent 的骨干模型，并在主要结果中使用 Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct [40] 作为辅助模型。文本生成采用最大 81,920 个 token，温度系数为 0.7，top_p 为 0.8，top_k 为 20，重复惩罚为 1.05。网络搜索和页面浏览通过 Google Serper API 实现，并

                                                                                                                                             

                                                                                                                                            Figure 4: Visualization of training dynamics, including (a) reward scores and (b) validation scores across training steps. 图 4：训练动态可视化，包括训练步数下的 (a) 奖励分数和 (b) 验证分数。

                                                                                                                                            Table 3: Ablation studies on the components of DeepAgent, where the best results are in bold. 表 3：DeepAgent 组件的消融实验，最优结果已加粗。

                                                                                                                                            MethodTool-UsageApplicationAvg.
                                                                                                                                            ToolB.ToolH.WebS.GAIA
                                                                                                                                            DeepAgent-32B-RL64.040.634.453.348.1

                                                                                                                                            w/o Tool Simulation62.035.233.648.544.8

                                                                                                                                            方法工具使用应用平均值
                                                                                                                                            ToolB.ToolH.WebS.GAIA
                                                                                                                                            DeepAgent-32B-RL64.040.634.453.348.1
                                                                                                                                            无训练（基座）60.038.432.046.744.3

                                                                                                                                            无工具优势归因62.039.633.249.546.1

                                                                                                                                            Jina Reader API, respectively. The VQA tool is based on Qwen2.5- VL-32B-Instruct [1]. Tool retrieval is performed using bge-large-en-v1.5 [62]. Training consists of 100 steps of ToolPO with batch size 64,λ1=λ2=1 ,rollout size K=8 ,and maximum sequence length 32,768. Additional details are provided in Appendix B. All experiments are conducted on 64 NVIDIA H20-141GB GPUs. Jina Reader API。VQA工具基于Qwen2.5-VL-32B-Instruct [1]。工具检索使用bge-large-en-v1.5 [62]。训练包含100步ToolPO，批大小为64,λ1=λ2=1，展开大小为K=8，最大序列长度为32,768。更多细节见附录B。所有实验均在64块NVIDIA H20-141GB GPU上进行。

                                                                                                                                            5 Experimental Results

                                                                                                                                            5 实验结果

                                                                                                                                            5.1 Main Results on General Tool Usage Tasks

                                                                                                                                            5.1 通用工具使用任务的主要结果

                                                                                                                                            Table 1 summarizes results on general tool-use tasks and yields three observations. (1) DeepAgent's End-to-End Reasoning Surpasses Workflow-Based Methods. DeepAgent consistently outperforms workflow-based agents. On labeled-tool tasks, DeepAgent- 32B-RL reaches 89.0% on TMDB and 75.4% on Spotify, exceeding the best 32B baselines (55.0% and 52.6%). This highlights the advantage of end-to-end agentic reasoning over rigid, predefined action loops. (2) DeepAgent Maintains Robustness in Open-Set Scenarios. Gains are larger in open-set settings where tool discovery is required: on ToolBench and ToolHop, DeepAgent-32B-RL achieves 64.0% and 40.6%, surpassing the best baselines (54.0% and 29.0%). This suggests that on-demand tool discovery within the reasoning process is both more robust and more scalable in realistic open-set tool environments. (3) ToolPO Training Further Improves Tool-Usage Capabilities. ToolPO yields consistent improvements over the base model, increasing ToolBench success by up to 6.0% and Spotify (labeled) by 5.2%. These gains indicate that our RL training better aligns intermediate tool calls with end-task success. 表1总结了通用工具使用任务的结果，得出三点观察。（1）DeepAgent的端到端推理优于基于工作流的方法。DeepAgent始终优于基于工作流的智能体。在标记工具任务中，DeepAgent-32B-RL在TMDB和Spotify上分别达到89.0%和75.4%，超过了最佳32B基线（55.0%和52.6%）。这凸显了端到端智能体推理相较于僵化、预定义动作循环的优势。（2）DeepAgent在开放集场景中保持稳健性。在需要工具发现的开放集设置中，增益更为显著：在ToolBench和ToolHop上，DeepAgent-32B-RL分别达到64.0%和40.6%，超过了最佳基线（54.0%和29.0%）。这表明在推理过程中按需发现工具在现实的开放集工具环境中更稳健且更具可扩展性。（3）ToolPO训练进一步提升了工具使用能力。ToolPO在基础模型上实现了持续改进，ToolBench成功率最高提升6.0%，Spotify（标记）提升5.2%。这些增益表明我们的强化学习训练能更好地将中间工具调用与最终任务成功对齐。

                                                                                                                                            Table 4: Effectiveness analysis of autonomous tool retrieval strategy in open-set scenarios compared to pre-retrieved tool methods. Numbers in parentheses indicate toolset sizes. 表4：在开放集场景中，自主工具检索策略与预检索工具方法的有效性分析。括号中的数字表示工具集大小。

                                                                                                                                            MethodToolB. (16k)ToolH. (3.9k)TMDB (54)Spotify (40)Avg.
                                                                                                                                            ReAct Workflow
